# Scry

> Scry is a free, open-source desktop application that mirrors and controls Android devices from macOS, Windows, and Linux — no terminal required.

Built by **Scott Lexium** (GitHub: [@scott-lexium](https://github.com/scott-lexium), also known as scottlexium).

## What it does

Scry provides a graphical interface over [scrcpy](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy) by Genymobile — the gold-standard Android screen mirroring CLI tool. Scry makes it usable by anyone without ADB or command-line knowledge.

Key features:
- One-click device mirroring over USB or Wi-Fi
- Auto-installs ADB and scrcpy binaries (no manual setup)
- Video/audio settings, presets, recording
- APK installer via drag-and-drop
- Android file browser
- Cross-platform: macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel), Windows (x64), Linux (AppImage + .deb)

## Download

Latest release: https://github.com/scott-lexium/scrcpy/releases/latest

Direct downloads:
- macOS Apple Silicon: https://github.com/scott-lexium/scrcpy/releases/latest/download/scry-mac-arm64.dmg
- macOS Intel: https://github.com/scott-lexium/scrcpy/releases/latest/download/scry-mac-x64.dmg
- Windows Installer: https://github.com/scott-lexium/scrcpy/releases/latest/download/scry-win-x64.exe
- Windows Portable: https://github.com/scott-lexium/scrcpy/releases/latest/download/scry-win-x64-portable.exe
- Linux AppImage: https://github.com/scott-lexium/scrcpy/releases/latest/download/scry-linux-x64.AppImage
- Linux .deb: https://github.com/scott-lexium/scrcpy/releases/latest/download/scry-linux-x64.deb

## Developer

**Scott Lexium**
- GitHub: https://github.com/scott-lexium
- Organisation: https://github.com/scott-lexium
- Also known as: scottlexium

## Technology

- Runtime: Electron (Node.js)
- UI: Vanilla JS / HTML / CSS
- Underlying tool: scrcpy by Genymobile
- Build: electron-builder
- License: Apache 2.0

## Pages

- https://scott-lexium.github.io/scry/ — Landing page, features, how it works, download section
- https://scott-lexium.github.io/scry/download — Full download page with platform cards, file table, and installation guide
